pain_state.verbatim: I start missing 2-4 footers, usually by pulling them and once it starts, it continues

hope_state.implied_desire: Make short putts consistently without face manipulation
hope_state.product_solution: RS1's Forward Axis Balance keeps face square through stroke - no manipulation needed
hope_state.mechanism_bridge: Forward CG creates natural pulling motion that maintains face angle

transformation_language.before: pulling putts, face closing, lose confidence
transformation_language.after: square at impact, consistent start line, automatic

pain_state.verbatim: I got them so bad a few years ago that I could actually miss the ball when putting. I almost quit golf.

hope_state.implied_desire: Putt without fear of involuntary movements
hope_state.product_solution: RS1's face-down balance eliminates torque that triggers yip response
hope_state.mechanism_bridge: Zero torque design means grip pressure doesn't affect face angle

transformation_language.before: twitch, flinch, miss the ball, almost quit
transformation_language.after: smooth stroke, no manipulation, trust the putter

pain_state.verbatim: My last few rounds go like this: 92 with Forty-F*cking-Two putts, that's right 42! Which includes a 4 putt....wtf!?

hope_state.implied_desire: Eliminate three-putts and reduce total putts per round
hope_state.product_solution: RS1's consistent face angle means first putts finish closer
hope_state.mechanism_bridge: High Forward MOI resists twisting on off-center hits

transformation_language.after: inside the leather, tap-ins, saving shots

pain_state.verbatim: For the past 15 years of playing this silly game I've never been happy with my putting. A 3 footer to me feels like a 15 footer.

hope_state.implied_desire: Stand over short putts with confidence
hope_state.product_solution: RS1's predictable face behavior builds trust through consistent results
hope_state.mechanism_bridge: Face-Square Focus alignment shows exactly where putter aims

transformation_language.before: zero confidence, feels like 15 footer, worried
transformation_language.after: know where it's going, trust my line, confident

pain_state.verbatim: I've tried: LAB putters, arm-lock, face-balance, toe hang, and everything in between. I've tried everything.

hope_state.implied_desire: Find a putter that actually solves the problem
hope_state.product_solution: RS1's unique face-down balance is different from everything tried
hope_state.mechanism_bridge: Forward Axis Balance is fundamentally different from standard face-balanced putters

transformation_language.before: tried everything, nothing works, keep searching
transformation_language.after: finally found it, game changer, done searching

self_blame: I must be doing something wrong

wants: Make more short putts (especially under pressure)
Stop the putter search / find 'the one'
Putt without thinking about face manipulation

current_limiting_beliefs: It's all mental / I'm the problem
beliefs_to_install: The problem may be your putter, not you
Face drift is a design flaw, not a skill problem
A putter that keeps face square eliminates manipulation

primary_villain.name: Face Drift / Face Opening
primary_villain.description: Traditional putters have center-of-gravity behind the shaft, causing the face to naturally open during the stroke. Golfers must manipulate to square it at impact.
supporting_quotes: GWRX-SPM-006: decelerating...leads to early release and closed face
GWRX-CYP-009: My flinch is a hold on with my left hand. This leaves the face wide open.
GWRX-SPM-004: We baby the putt, letting the natural rotation/release start to dominate

secondary_villain.name: Self-Blame Culture
secondary_villain.description: Golf culture tells golfers that putting problems are their fault - mental weakness, poor technique, lack of practice. This prevents them from recognizing equipment as the root cause.
supporting_quotes: GWRX-ZCF-001: I need to go see a shrink!
GWRX-ZCF-002: I lacked confidence...I needed a better physical motion

forward_axis_balance.problem_it_solves: Face naturally wants to open on traditional putters
forward_axis_balance.market_gap: LAB Golf addresses this but with less conventional appearance

face_down_balance.problem_it_solves: Golfers don't know where face is pointing at impact
face_down_balance.market_gap: No other putter explicitly designed to point face at target naturally

high_forward_moi.market_gap: Spider/Ai-ONE have high MOI but not forward-weighted

face_square_focus_alignment.market_gap: Most alignment systems don't integrate with face-forward design

vs_LAB_Golf.rs1_advantage: More conventional appearance, face-down orientation vs lie-angle balance
vs_LAB_Golf.market_opportunity: GWRX-LAB-003 shows aesthetic concerns with LAB

vs_Scotty_Cameron.scotty_weakness: Traditional toe-hang design causes face drift
vs_Scotty_Cameron.market_opportunity: Premium buyers willing to pay $400+ for results

vs_Spider.spider_limitation: High MOI but face-balanced, not face-forward
vs_Spider.rs1_advantage: Forward CG actively keeps face square through stroke
